Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: I've lived a simple life in Philadelphia all my life, working as a plumber and enjoying my hobbies like photography and rock climbing. To be honest, I don't understand this social media business, and I'm glad I don't have to deal with it. But I know some people struggle with the fake content on these platforms. From what I've observed, it's easy to get caught up in the drama and misinformation. I think it's essential to be critical and discerning when consuming content online. Don't take everything at face value; verify the information through reputable sources. It's also crucial to maintain a healthy online presence by limiting your screen time and avoiding engaging with toxic individuals. If you're feeling overwhelmed, take a break and focus on real-life connections. I've seen people get so caught up in their online personas that they forget about the beauty of living in the present moment. In my experience, a balanced life is key to happiness, and social media shouldn't be a priority. I'm not a tech expert, but I believe it's essential to be mindful of our online interactions and to prioritize authenticity over curated perfection. I've seen the impact of fake content on people's mental health, and it's heartbreaking. We should strive to create a positive online environment that promotes understanding, empathy, and respect. I'm not sure if I'll ever join the social media bandwagon, but I'll continue to encourage others to prioritize their well-being and authenticity.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I based my opinion on my life experiences and observations. As a simple man who values authenticity and real-life connections, I believe it's essential to be critical and discerning when consuming content online. I also drew from my understanding of the potential negative impacts of fake content on mental health. I didn't rely on any specific studies or research, but rather on my own experiences and common sense. My tone is straightforward and honest, reflecting my personality as a straightforward and no-nonsense plumber.
